articles.title: Graph of the Day: Batteries overtake gas in morning peaks in Australia’s most advanced renewable grid
articles.summary:
Batteries are delivering higher peak morning supply than gas. Gas is still twice as large as batteries in the evening but it's losing share. 
The post Graph of the Day: Batteries overtake gas in morning peaks in Australia’s most advanced renewable grid appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: How does Australia compare on wind turbine density? The answer might surprise you
articles.summary:
How does Australia compare to the rest of the world on the number wind turbines per 1000km squared? 
The post How does Australia compare on wind turbine density? The answer might surprise you appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Why an oil crisis is bad news for Australia’s biggest coal state – and how to break the cycle
articles.summary:
One state in Australia remains particularly vulnerable to global oil shocks because it hasn't built the replacement capacity for coal. Batteries will help – and much more wind.
The post Why an oil crisis is bad news for Australia’s biggest coal state – and how to break the cycle appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Energy Insiders Podcast: How the world’s fourth biggest economy plans to reach 100 pct clean energy
articles.summary:
David Hochschild, the head of the California Energy Commission, on how the world's fourth biggest economy is moving away from fossil fuels, despite Trump. Plus: News of the week.
The post Energy Insiders Podcast: How the world’s fourth biggest economy plans to reach 100 pct clean energy appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: When will the energy sector understand the National Energy Objective? When will governments enforce its intent?
articles.summary:
Fifty years of cheap gas and electricity and intensive marketing have distorted perceptions. Every element of our electricity market is now being challenged by reality.
The post When will the energy sector understand the National Energy Objective? When will governments enforce its intent? appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: “It is paramount:” AEMO says system and market operator functions must be kept together
articles.summary:
Australian Energy Market Operator says its system and market operation functions should not be separated amid an in-depth review of its governance arrangements.
The post “It is paramount:” AEMO says system and market operator functions must be kept together appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Powerful new rooftop solar panel promises system sizes “previously out of reach”
articles.summary:
The Clean Energy Council has approved a new PV module with around 25 per cent more generating capacity than most of its competitors – promising more power over less rooftop space.
The post Powerful new rooftop solar panel promises system sizes “previously out of reach” appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Webinar: The new era of home energy storage in Australia
articles.summary:
An in-depth webinar exploring the next phase of residential battery storage in Australia, brought to you by GoodWe. 
The post Webinar: The new era of home energy storage in Australia appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: A battery with every turbine: Huge wind project seeks federal green tick with novel storage plan
articles.summary:
Huge wind project that survived LNP's perilous project call-in process kicks off full federal environmental assessment, with novel plan for battery with every turbine. 
The post A battery with every turbine: Huge wind project seeks federal green tick with novel storage plan appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Offshore wind installation tech promises to change logistics game, cut reliance on deep-water ports
articles.summary:
A potentially game-changing offshore wind turbine installation solution is set to make its way to Australia with help from local renewables outfit Energy Estate.
The post Offshore wind installation tech promises to change logistics game, cut reliance on deep-water ports appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Remote Samoan island communities to receive Australian-backed rooftop solar
articles.summary: Remote Samoan island communities will receive roof top solar, battery storage and a mini-grid upgrade to cut dependence on diesel, kerosene, wood fires and disposable batteries for light and cooking, with the Australian government's REnew Pacific assistance.

articles.title: First Nations clean energy strategy implementation funds now available
articles.summary: Australian government grants are now available for First Nations communities, organisations and groups for advice and engagement on planning, negotiating or pursuing clean energy projects in homes, communities and on Country.

articles.title: VicGrid tenders for three Latrobe Valley-based synchronous condensors
articles.summary: VicGrid is inviting expressions of interest from developers to partner in the delivery of at least three new synchronous condensers at or near the Hazelwood terminal station in the Latrobe Valley.

articles.title: Edify Energy Burroway solar farm and BESS lands NSW green light
articles.summary: Edify Energy's proposed Burroway 100 MW solar farm and 400 MWh battery energy storage system in the Central-West Orana Renewable Energy Zone has been approved by the NSW government, subject to development consent conditions.

articles.title: New York leads US in BESS moratoriums, recent data shows
articles.summary: New York, US is leading the country in battery energy storage system (BESS) moratoriums, with 97 moratoriums in place, according to renewables service provider Carina Energy.

articles.title: Google completes US$4.75 billion Intersect Power acquisition, initial focus on California & Texas solar-plus-storage
articles.summary: Google has finalised its acquisition of US renewable energy developer Intersect Power, as part of its plan to power its data centre expansions in the US.

articles.title: Flow battery startup ESS Tech Inc looks to acquisitions, big-name US partnerships for hope in future profitability
articles.summary: Iron flow battery company ESS Tech Inc highlighted its recent partnership with Google and the acquisition of VoltStorage GmbH, as profitability remained out of reach in 2025.

articles.title: ‘You need a clear RFQ to get clear offers’: Procuring BESS at scale with European IPP R.Power
articles.summary: European IPP R.Power’s head of BESS and CCO sat down with Energy-Storage.news at the Energy Storage Summit 2026 last month for a video interview to discuss the firm's storage activity and approach to procuring technology.

articles.title: MGA Thermal raises AU$17 million as thermal energy storage startup enters commercial scale-up phase
articles.summary: Australia’s MGA Thermal has secured AU$17 million (US$12 million) in new investment for its long-duration thermal energy storage technology as it enters the commercial scale-up phase.

articles.title: JinkoSolar inks 2 GW Tiger Neo supply deal with Blue Sun Group
articles.summary: Chinese clean energy technology manufacturer JinkoSolar has signed a memorandum of understanding with Blue Sun Group to supply 2 GW of high-efficiency Tiger Neo 3.0 modules to the Australian market.

articles.title: GoodWe introduces low-noise all-in-one storage system for rooftop solar
articles.summary: The three phase energy storage system encompasses the inverter, battery and energy management into a single system, suited for residential customers and scalable to the small-scale commercial and industrial market.

articles.title: Clean industry transition is $300 billion opportunity in the making
articles.summary: Australia’s transition to clean industry could unlock approximately $300 billion in economic opportunity if action is taken to deliver a pipeline of 70 projects, according to a new briefing from two global alliances pushing to decarbonise large scale industry.

articles.title: Scientists identify overlooked step in lithium-ion battery recycling
articles.summary: New research by Monash University identifies pre-treatment of lithium-ion batteries as an overlooked recycling strategy in the recovery of valuable metals lithium, nickel, and cobalt.

articles.title: Floating solar to cut diesel dependence for remote Asia-Pacific islands
articles.summary: An international collaboration plans to set up floating solar microgrids in remote Asia-Pacific locations to charge high-tech electric hydrofoil ferries and help island communities break diesel fuel dependence.

articles.title: Foresight invests $500 million to acquire New Zealand Clean Energy
articles.summary: Investment heavyweight Foresight Group has entered the New Zealand market for the first time with a $500 million commitment to acquire local developer New Zealand Clean Energy and its extended portfolio that includes three ready-to-build solar and BESS projects.

articles.title: New Jersey state government advances 1GW of battery storage in energy affordability push
articles.summary: New Jersey's regulator has approved the construction of battery storage facilities and a procurement for further energy storage resources in the PJM-connected US state.

articles.title: US BESS industry is ‘primed for years of growth’: Enertis Applus+ on financing storage in the FEOC era
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news speaks to Willliam Lauwers, head of technology, BESS, at consulting, engineering and quality control firm Enertis Applus+, ahead of the upcoming Energy Storage Summit USA.

articles.title: US Roundup: Over US$2 billion of financing updates from Arevon, Primergy and Gridstor
articles.summary: In this US news roundup, we have financing updates for GridStor, Arevon, and Primergy, related to energy storage projects in Texas, California, and Nevada, respectively.

articles.title: EU Roundup: 700MWh of BESS projects move forward in Germany, Belgium, France, Finland, Latvia and Portugal
articles.summary: Another busy week of BESS news from across Europe, with investors and owner-operators ABO, Field, Aspiravi, Prime Capital, Latvenergo, Sonnedix and Amarenco progressing large-scale projects across Europe.

articles.title: What makes a ‘priority market’ for scaling long-duration energy storage?
articles.summary: The LDES Council’s policy team discusses the role of policy and markets in scaling long-duration energy storage globally with ESN Premium.

articles.title: AGL’s Torrens Island BESS in South Australia hit by a second AEMO direction
articles.summary: Australia's battery energy storage sector faces mounting operational pressures, following the Australian Energy Market Operator (AEMO) issuing its latest direction to AGL's Torrens Island battery energy storage system (BESS) on 9 March.

articles.title: Aiko launches 545 W back-contact solar module with 25% efficiency
articles.summary: The Chinese manufacturer said its new back-contact module delivers up to 545 W and over 25% efficiency in a compact rooftop format for residential, C&I, and off-grid applications.

articles.title: Survey shows technology trends reshaping utility-scale solar
articles.summary: Bifacial solar panels, string inverters and advanced tracking systems are fast becoming the technologies of choice for developers of large-scale solar according to new data provided by Spain-based software company and analytics provider RatedPower.

articles.title: Why grid-forming BESS is becoming the preferred solution for hyperscale data centres
articles.summary: Australia has rapidly become one of the world’s most attractive destination for hyperscale data centres, ranking within the top 5-10 markets globally by capacity. Several factors explain this rise: large land availability within Renewable Energy Zones, fast-growing renewable penetration surpassing 40% across the National Electricity Market, geopolitical stability, Australia’s status as a trusted Five Eyes jurisdiction, and proximity to Asia-Pacific demand centres.

articles.title: Octopus breaks ground on Blind Creek solar and battery project
articles.summary: Construction has officially started on Octopus Australia’s Blind Creek Solar Farm and battery project in southern New South Wales, one of the biggest DC-coupled hybrid projects being developed in the country.

articles.title: BESS asset operation in CAISO is complex, but lean teams can leverage advanced analytics
articles.summary: California's rapid adoption of energy storage has been a major success, but market participation is complex and competitive, write Matt Drazenovich of TWAICE and Chris Swanson of Fullmark Energy.

articles.title: RWE brings online four BESS projects in 13GW US clean energy portfolio
articles.summary: Energy firm RWE added 2GW to its US operating capacity in 2025, bringing its total operational capacity to nearly 13GW across the country.

articles.title: Aypa Power closes US$512 million financing for two Ontario BESS projects
articles.summary: Energy storage developer-operator Aypa Power, and Six Nations of the Grand River Development Corporation (SNGRDC) have closed CA$700 million (US$512 million) for two battery energy storage system (BESS) projects in Ontario, Canada.

articles.title: Google bets big on 30GWh of Form Energy’s iron-air battery storage despite efficiency trade-offs
articles.summary: Recently, utility Xcel Energy announced it would install 30GWh of US startup Form Energy’s iron-air batteries at a data centre in Pine Island, Minnesota, owned by tech giant Google.

articles.title: FREE DOWNLOAD: The Energy Storage Report 2026
articles.summary: The Energy Storage Report 2026 is packed with insights, analysis and fresh reporting on the headline topics in BESS today.

articles.title: Price jump: Chinese firms’ battery cell quotes approach 0.4 RMB/Wh, system prices follow suit
articles.summary: China’s energy storage sector may be at the start of a price trend reversal, with recent data showing price increases.

articles.title: Longi launches fire-resistant solar module for rooftop PV
articles.summary: The Chinese manufacturer has launched a fire-resistant version of its Hi-MO X10 module for distributed PV applications, featuring back-contact technology and up to 24.8% efficiency. The company says the module adds enhanced fire-safety design to address rooftop PV risks such as hot spots and DC arcing.

articles.title: Why CIGS solar keeps lagging behind crystalline silicon
articles.summary: In an interview with pv magazine, Empa scientist Mirjana Dimitrievska explains that the CIGS photovoltaic technology, while promising for efficiency and flexible applications, faces persistent challenges in scaling lab-level performance to cost-effective, high-throughput industrial production, limiting its ability to compete with crystalline silicon. She also emphasizes that long-term stability, reliability, and material sustainability, rather than peak efficiency, are crucial for commercial ...

articles.title: Solar ingot and wafer manufacturing plans given major project status
articles.summary: Plans to build Australia’s first large-scale silicon ingot and wafer manufacturing facility in Queensland have been put on the fast track by the federal government in a nod to the project’s potential to play a key role in supporting a domestic solar supply chain.

articles.title: Potentia reaches construction milestone on NEM-first solar-battery hybrid project
articles.summary: Potentia Energy has completed the installation of more than 161,000 solar modules at its Quorn Park hybrid solar-plus-storage project in western New South Wales, marking a new milestone in Australia’s renewable energy transition.

articles.title: Argentina launches 700MW/2,800MWh BESS tender
articles.summary: The Argentinian Government has launched a new battery energy storage system (BESS) tender seeking 700MW and 4-hour duration.

articles.title: Long-duration tech still outcompeted by lithium, trade group advocates for ‘valuing diversity’
articles.summary: Global long-duration energy storage (LDES) deployment figures soared in 2025, but newer technologies risk being outcompeted by the incumbent, lithium-ion (Li-ion).

articles.title: IPP Econergy’s head of energy storage talks pipeline, financing, Germany, procurement and pricing
articles.summary: Joshua Murphy, head of energy storage for IPP Econergy, met with Energy-Storage.news for a video interview to discuss various business and financial topics at the Energy Storage Summit 2026 in London last month.

articles.title: Base Power launches 100MW VPP programme in Texas
articles.summary: Residential energy storage startup Base Power is collaborating with utility Denton County Electric Cooperative (CoServ) to deploy 100MW of residential energy storage across North Texas, US.

articles.title: From software to shareholder: Flower, Volkswagen’s Elli and Nuvve launch Germany &  Sweden BESS projects
articles.summary: Software and optimisation firms Flower, Volkswagen's Elli and Nuvve have progressed large-scale BESS projects under own-and-operate models; Flower and Elli in Germany and the latter in Sweden.

articles.title: Octopus Australia breaks ground on 486MWh Blind Creek Solar Farm and Battery project
articles.summary: Octopus Australia has broken ground on the 486MWh Blind Creek Solar Farm and Battery site in New South Wales (NSW).

articles.title: Australia: NSW planners approve 400MWh Burroway solar-plus-storage despite community objections
articles.summary: The NSW Independent Planning Commission has approved Edify Energy's 400MWh Burroway solar-plus-storage project in Australia.

articles.title: Solis launches new residential storage system portfolio
articles.summary: The Chinese manufacturer said its new three residential battery lines support a depth of discharge of 90% and deliver over 6,000 cycles.

articles.title: Energy Storage Inspection 2026: Fox ESS, SMA, SAX Power, Kostal and BYD impress with high efficiency
articles.summary: The energy storage efficiency of 12 solar storage systems with capacities of 5 kW or 10 kW has been put to the test, including several new products, all of which achieved efficiency class A. The latest interation of the annual study also examined efficiency losses when storing electricity from the grid and took a closer look at the warranty conditions of the devices.

articles.title: Rooftop solar volumes rebound as home batteries continue to boom
articles.summary: Australia’s rooftop solar market has bounced back into record-setting territory with 281 MW of new capacity registered across the country last month, marking the largest February total on record.

articles.title: How graphene could power the frontlines
articles.summary: Nearly 20 years after the first perovskite solar cell was created, the efficiency and durability of this small but highly efficient film have attracted attention from industries around the world. Front of the queue is the global defence industry, where rising geopolitical tensions are resulting in record spending by governments to boost capabilities and protect critical systems from attack, whether from natural disasters or more sinister threats like military conflict.

articles.title: Fortescue begins building WA’s biggest solar farm
articles.summary: Fortescue has begun construction of Western Australia’s biggest solar farm to date as the company pushes ahead with plans to decarbonise its Australian iron ore operations by the end of the decade.

articles.title: Hanjung America to build ESS manufacturing facility in Indiana to support StarPlus Energy
articles.summary: Hanjung America, a subsidiary of South Korea-based battery manufacturer Hanjung NCS, will build its first US energy storage system (ESS) manufacturing facility in Huntington, Indiana, US.

articles.title: US import tariffs ‘increase the policy risk’ for BESS
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news speaks with Isshu Kikuma, energy storage analyst at BloombergNEF (BNEF), ahead of the upcoming Energy Storage Summit USA.

articles.title: EU ‘Made in Europe’ regulation welcomed but ‘will not remove dependency on China’
articles.summary: A European Commission (EC) proposal to add domestic content requirements for energy storage will ‘significantly impact’ the industry but may not reduce overall dependence on China.

articles.title: Origin Energy’s 650MWh grid-forming BESS begins commissioning in Australia
articles.summary: Origin Energy's 300MW/650MWh Mortlake BESS has entered AEMO's MMS, marking the facility’s transition into its commissioning phase.

articles.title: Tigo debuts 725 W optimiser for high-power solar modules
articles.summary: The new product has a maximum wattage of 725 W, a maximum current of 22 A, and a maximum efficiency of 99.6%.

articles.title: 198,000 rooftop solar panels help Woolworths achieve 100% clean power
articles.summary: Australian retail giant Woolworths Group has announced it is 100% powered by renewable electricity across its superkmarkets, distribution centres, BIG W stores and support offices in Australia and New Zealand.

articles.title: Sungrow and Delta Capacity sign 1GWh European framework BESS supply deal
articles.summary: Inverter and BESS firm Sungrow and BESS owner-operator Delta Capacity signed a 1GWh framework supply agreement for European projects at the Energy Storage Summit 2026 in London last week.

articles.title: Thailand’s energy storage market lags despite renewable push and upstream manufacturing support
articles.summary: Thailand is increasing its renewable generation but its energy storage market still lags behind, while there is support for the upstream industry, writes analyst Charlotte Gisbourne.

articles.title: Australia: Construction starts on Hunter-Central Coast REZ in New South Wales
articles.summary: Construction has officially commenced on a network upgrade in Australia’s New South Wales Upper Hunter region, which is set to boost transfer capacity by at least 1GW by 2028, in support of the state’s renewable energy transition.

articles.title: Australia’s Cheaper Home Batteries Program reportedly under review as government seeks budget savings
articles.summary: The Australian government is reportedly exploring additional modifications to its Cheaper Home Batteries Program, including the possibility of an early wind-up, as the government seeks budget savings ahead of the May federal budget.

articles.title: Avantus secures US$300 million for 400MWh Arizona solar-plus-storage project
articles.summary: Developer Avantus has closed a financing package of over US$300 million for the 100MWac/130MWdc solar, 400MWh BESS Kitt Solar and Energy Storage Project in Pinal County, Arizona, US.

articles.title: Redwood Energy positions second-life BESS as ‘essential infrastructure’ for US data centre load growth, domestic manufacturing needs
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news Premium speaks with Claire McConnell, VP business development for Redwood Materials’ energy storage business, Redwood Energy, about its recent backing from Google and Nvidia, and what it has planned next.

articles.title: Bimergen acquires 79.2MW total of Texas BESS, Habitat advances Texas optimisation
articles.summary: From Texas, US, Bimergen Energy has acquired eight BESS projects totalling 79.2MW. Meanwhile, Habitat Energy announced its first US co-located solar-plus-storage partnership with Birch Creek.

articles.title: Grid-scale activity in Romania ramps up with gigawatts of announcements, led by Enery’s 1GWh financing
articles.summary: Large-scale BESS deployments in Romania are benefiting from country-specific drivers, helping Romania emerge as a busy grid-scale market.

articles.title: Policy and regulation risks are a common theme in Europe and US energy storage markets
articles.summary: In this second part of our interview with Wood Mackenzie energy storage analysts, we look at risk factors and mitigation across the European and US markets.

articles.title: How Australia can turn the data centre boom into a grid growth story
articles.summary: Jeff Monday of Fluence believes Australia can transform its data centre boom from a grid constraint into a growth opportunity using BESS.

articles.title: Fluence completes ahead-of-schedule delivery of all enclosures to 600MWh BESS in Australia
articles.summary: Fluence has delivered all battery enclosures for the 600MWh Bulabul Battery storage project in New South Wales ahead of schedule.

articles.title: New providers, new structures drive evolution of BESS offtake in Europe
articles.summary: We heard from Danske Commodities' principal originator Rimshah Javed at the Energy Storage Summit 2026, to discuss trends in BESS offtake, optimisation, FCAs in Germany and the Danish market. The latter has taken off in the past year.

articles.title: Jupiter Power receives approval for 2.8GWh Massachusetts BESS
articles.summary: Utility-scale battery storage developer Jupiter Power’s 700MW/2,800MWh Trimount battery energy storage system (BESS) in Everett, Massachusetts, US, has been approved by state officials.

articles.title: Europe and the US: Comparing and contrasting two maturing energy storage markets
articles.summary: ESN Premium takes advantage of an opportunity to compare Europe and US energy storage markets with Wood Mackenzie energy storage analysts.

articles.title: Australia’s NSW to unleash AU$34.4 billion energy investment blitz through fast-track authority
articles.summary: The NSW government has endorsed 16 projects worth a collective AU$34.4 billion through its newly established Investment Delivery Authority.

articles.title: Australia’s ACT government completes its first community battery network with new 450kWh system
articles.summary: The Australian Capital Territory (ACT) has completed its first network of community batteries with the commissioning of a 450kWh system in Dickson.

articles.title: Australia: Edify Energy secures EPC contractor for 3.6GWh of solar-plus-storage; Flow Power acquires 60MW hybrid project
articles.summary: 3.6GWh of solar-plus-storage developments have progressed in Australia this week, with Edify Energy partnering with DT Infrastructure and Flow Power acquiring a 60MW project.

articles.title: Utility Portland General Electric finalises agreements for 1GW renewables and BESS
articles.summary: Oregon-based utility company Portland General Electric has finalised agreements for more than 1,000MW of new renewable energy and BESS projects.

articles.title: Puerto Rico: Polaris Renewable Energy receives approval for 71.4MW BESS projects
articles.summary: The Governing Board of the Puerto Rico Electric Power Authority (PREPA) has approved renewables developer Polaris Renewable Energy’s battery energy storage system (BESS) standard offer (SO1) agreement.

articles.title: UK: Grid-forming batteries miss out in system stability tender as synchronous condensers, gas get contracts
articles.summary: A second-round auction in the UK for grid stability services, including inertia, concluded without any wins for grid-forming battery energy storage system (BESS) projects.

articles.title: Allianz GI, OX2, Luxcara, Return, Low Carbon, Cero, Revera invest in BESS across Europe, totalling 3.7GWh
articles.summary: Allianz GI, Luxcara and Return have acquired BESS projects and portfolios in Germany, Finland and Spain; Low Carbon, OX2, Cero Generation and Revera have taken FIDs on projects in Poland, Finland and the UK; and NHOA Energy has received a 600MWh order in Italy. All in all, the projects total at least 3.7GWh of capacity.

articles.title: Chinese energy storage players’ 2025 earnings forecasts: High growth, turnarounds and profit declines
articles.summary: A number of Chinese energy storage companies have released their 2025 annual earnings results and forecasts, revealing divergent performance.
